agent_outputs.raw: The memo claims end-of-term learning gains but the evidence only shows short-horizon simulator performance. These are fundamentally different outcomes. The RL policy may optimize for worked-example reliance, which produces high immediate accuracy but suppresses retrieval practice and spacing effects that drive retention. The simulator also can't model what happens when students enter content states the logging policy rarely visited. Engagement, frustration, and teacher overrides further affect end-of-term outcomes in ways the simulator doesn't capture. Critique wins.

Three gaps between evidence and claim. One: immediate task accuracy is not end-of-term learning. Two: simulator built on logging-policy trajectories may not be reliable for the new policy's state distribution. Three: engagement effects and teacher overrides determine whether students reach the end of term with enough practice to show gains. None of these are captured. The evidence supports a pilot, not a launch with the stated claim.

The adaptive tutoring policy shows promising results in simulation — 8 percentage points higher immediate accuracy and improved offline reward. But the launch memo claims end-of-term learning gains, which requires delayed assessment evidence. Immediate correctness is a weak proxy for retention and transfer. The simulator may have coverage gaps for the new policy. Engagement and dropout are unmonitored. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. Three agents independently identify the same gaps between the simulation evidence and the end-of-term learning claim. All three conclude a randomized pilot with delayed outcomes is needed.

etd_constraint.condition: Randomized classroom pilot with delayed post-test and end-of-unit assessments; dropout and teacher override tracking
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: No delayed learning improvement or worsened engagement under new policy
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Significant end-of-unit or end-of-term learning gains with stable engagement
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Some assessment gains but dropout increases or effects vary by teacher/student subgroup

all_issues_raised: Immediate accuracy not long-term learning (all agents)
Simulator coverage gaps for new-policy states (all agents)
Engagement and teacher override effects absent (all agents)
